item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just wanna point out that the image was an edited version of a picture of a bog mummy with a canine mummy skull edited in, like these:

+ show block
– hide block

https://www.google.com/search?hl=en&tbm=isch&source=hp&biw=1366&bih=641&ei=v_EUXqfLLqa4ggeCpLLQDg&q=bog+mummy&oq=bog+mummy&gs_l=img.1.0.0l5j0i5i30l2j0i8i30j0i24l2.14934.17270..18948...0.0..0.93.516.9......0....1..gws-wiz-img.......0i131j0i10.V4Tr2IJeglw

Collapsed the link so it wouldn't stretch screens. ~Zyn

Original, unedited picture is in the middle of the sixth row down in the image results.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image could not be sourced, as all possible links and domains that were connected to it have long since disappeared due to age. As such, the image is assumed not released under a license compliant with CC 3.0 and has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does anyone have the old image of 1991?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very interesting concept, with a very apparent and as of yet unknown backstory. I'd love to learn more about this creature, especially since it seems generally amicable when not feeling threatened. (most canid behavior wouldn't cause something to lick someone's hand from a simple 'hello')
+1 for potential, I'm dying to learn more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So sad, abused animals play my heartstrings like a fiddle. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Poor pupper :(

Upvoted.

E: I meant to type pupper

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Effect upon subject: SCP-1991 prodded toy with forepaw, causing it to squeak. SCP-1991 proceeded to emit whimpering noise and huddle against the furthest wall of the containment chamber until chew toy was removed.

That chew toy is clearly evil. +1 for that poor mistreated…um, thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Chupacabot Puppy!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aww, its like a sad robot kobold. +1

item.title: Re: It's Back!
item.content:encoded:
No unspoilered pictures in the forums.- Scantron

Offing of fed or female specimen?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels like a crossover between that girl with robotic implants (cant remember where it is) and SCP-1364.
It also isn't interesting enough on its own to get a +1, not to mention some tonal problems.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder about a potential connection to SCP-326.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For the record, this is based on a photo of Tollund Man. (Reverse GIS is really quite an impressive tool.)

item.title: Possible Rewrite
item.content:encoded:
Following reading this, I've decided that really, it's kind of flawed. The idea of "it's someone's abused cyborg weapon, which broke" isn't fully expressed by the report. In other words, I've been working on a minor re-write.

P.S. Sorry, but this really doesn't seem very much like a Serpent's Hand thing to me. The Hand would probably be nicer to it, whereas when the foundation finds it, it's broken, traumatized by abuse and starving. But I agree that they need to be in more things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, even if the feet look illustrated. The image, not the… nevermind.

A thought: why not use a second hand recovery? The Serpent's Hand is in need of some good story-loving1, and rescuing this poor creature seems like the thing they'd do. So long as you keep vague. Your thing and all, so do what you will.

Footnotes
1. That is to say, "I WANT TO SEE THEM IN MORE THINGS! MOAR!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
History: SCP-1991 was recovered by foundation Agents in ██/██/20██ in a raid on a Marshal, Carter and Dark auction in [DATA EXPUNGED]. Interrogation of surviving Marshal, Carter and Dark employees and customers revealed no information besides the existence of Document - 1991 - 3 claimed to have been recovered with SCP-1991 in [DATA EXPUNGED] by Marshal, Carter and Dark employees.

Document - 1991 - 3

need advice for this document

How's that for an alternate history for this guy? Once someone comes up with a good idea for the document.

EDIT: Also, I found a better picture. What do you think?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: see thats why i proposed a rocovery goi they wouldnt make a monster but they would use it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The problem with that is I really am trying to keep hidden all information about who or what made this guy.

Perhaps it was attacking a foundation site when fully functional and the present state is due to bullet damage inflicted by Agents?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. I'm not sure
2> USe a recovery GOI, lessee, MC&D are in need of a return but this is very chaos insurgency.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The two things that this idea still needs are in my opinion as the author.

1. A way of making clear that the collar was meant to control it, apparently for usage as a weapon.

2. A better history/recovery.

Any ideas for how to do either of these?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this idea, but I would still love more testing logs…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely better. I got some feels off this. The description feels very rushed, though, almost too clinical. Use some articles, for gosh sakes! Got my upvote this time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehh… better than before, but it's still Rex the cyberdog with PTSD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oviform or ovoid might be a better term than oval. For the lack of tail, maybe something like "the creature does not a tail" or " there is no evidence that this creature ever had a tail".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not ignoring you Lycan Therapy. Simply put, while creepy and great SCPfuel none of the images you posted really look anything like what I think this guy does.
But I might use the "mama" picture from the flesh that sings for one of my other ideas, a SCP changling…

Also, I found a picture. Following your input Lycan Therapy, I'm not posting what it is, only that I'm pretty sure it hasn't shown up in any movies.

P.S. if anyone can come up with a better history for this guy then what I had that doesn't spoil the questions of WTF happened to it, then please post ideas so I can edit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*kicks people out of the way*
I AM SO CLAIMING THAT FIRST ONE, DOWN DOWN DOWN, GO GO GO, MINE MINE MINE! Muahahahaha!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I meant the not using of pop culture images specifically.

That could work, but you'd have to acount for why it was covered in seaweed. Looking like it was made of plastic.

(WHAT ABOUT THE IMAGES I LINKED!? WHY ARE YOU IGNORING ME!?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Um. Yes. It is hard.

Sorry.

Maybe this?
http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-2111411/Is-legendary-chupacabra-Giant-fanged-mutant-hellbeast-washes-sea-shore-just-boiled-dog.html#ixzz1oR17EvV7

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No! No popular culture! This isn't hard!

Although the latter might work. If you hadn't, you know, stated what it was already. To be honest, images which rely on a misconception of what they are always piss me off (even if I did do it that myself one time with the Tsetse fly meconium). But then, others may think differently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A cropped version of this guy?
http://fc07.deviantart.net/fs71/i/2010/354/f/8/eyless_without_the_blood_by_puzzels-d359cna.jpg

Or perhaps a mole rat closeup.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh? Eh?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was actually a fairly well known release (since it butchered the source material and outraged millions of fans.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know! Sir! Sir! Pick me!

How about you edit this?

EDIT: Good god. NSFW, I should think. In fact, everything I link.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was from a live-action Doom idea someone had. Not that well known.

Sorry, but you try googling "eyeless monster." Most of the hits are either porn and/or nightmare fuel.

Nevertheless, I will continue to look.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: HERE YA' GO.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's from Doom, it's specifically the horribly tweaked Hell Knight enemy that tosses around the strong black (african american) character with the gattling gun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, I can't stress enough that you want to avoid major motion pictures, look for indie creations or lesser known asian/european/african/exotic films if you must.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I remember that from Torchwood…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
http://application.denofgeek.com/images/m/eyeless/doom.jpg
Maybe this instead?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would not use that picture, I would not recommend that anyone use a screencap from a major blockbuster film (unless their SCP is or pertains to that film.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
O.K.

I have the following changes in mind.

1. Change face from skull-like with mane, to human-like head with only a mouth and long hair. This will both solve the "looks like 682" problem, and I found a picture. I just need to crop, or photoshop out the eye on it's hand.
http://blogs.amctv.com/horror-hacker/clash-of-the-titans-two.jpg

2. "oval" = "egg shaped" - need more technical term needed.

3. There is no tail. Sorry, but the creature is tail-less. Again I would welcome a better way of putting that.

4. SCP-1991's behavior is comparable to an abused dog. I agree. I really had that sound stupid. Will fix ASAP.

Thank you for your input.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't feel like you have to tack stuff on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From top down:

  • Item is a living, conscious, sentient organism. It should have Euclid status. I know you want us to think it won't hurt anybody, but injured animals are always the most dangerous.
  • I want a picture for this thing. If you are going to use language like "[its] body core is roughly oval", a picture is definitely needed. (An oval is a 2 dimensional shape.)
  • Tone problems abound:

SCP-1991's behavior is comparable to an abused dog

Serial use of the word comparable, and it would be better to say that the item suffered psychological damage as a result of physical abuse or trauma. Avoid evoking too much pathos with your word choice.

There is no tail.

This line made me laugh out loud. "WHO WAS PHONE?" The whole physical description needs a lot of work, as you're describing a never-before-seen organism, and it is the weakest part of the article. Either find a very good picture, or rewrite this being more descriptive and clinical. Avoid comparisons; it's unprofessional and makes you sound like a valley girl short on vocabulary.

  • The last paragraph in the description reads funky. It's great if you can visualize the object when writing, but make sure we can too.
  • Test logs are a little vanilla. I expected a much more, um, different reaction from firing a weapon near this thing.
  • Is the history log relevant, or just like a trivia thing? It seems wasted, and you can add so much with just a little interesting background.

I want to see more of this. Literally. I want a picture, or at least a beefed-up description. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As for the picture looking like 682, sorry, I really wasn't thinking of that. I just aimed for a scary monster-face which didn't look human and apparently came to similar results. If enough people want me to change it, I will.

As for a twist I still am not sure I should add, how about this:
It was a weapon. The "collar" controlled its actions. Now that the "collar" is broken it no longer works.

But when it did….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bad wording on my part- I meant half its fur was gone. Although I am now imagining it in a bikini… Urgh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is better than it was, but I don't feel very strongly about it. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It reminds me of a mix between that cyber kid and the anti 682.

I'll downvote for now because I don't feel much for it, but probably change to and upvote later when I'm feeling less like an emotionless grump, heh.

But, I'd like to note, there's nothing here except 'feel bad for it'. I think if there was more than one way to view this, other than pathetic and sad, it'd be a lot more interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure if I read the old version, but this is pretty good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, as I did the previous version.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The head resembles a equine skull, apart from a thick mane, empty eye sockets and elongated incisor teeth. There is no tail. Skin resembles decayed leather with matted fur growing from it.

This sounds a lot like a description of the picture for SCP-682.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
half-naked

Does it have partial clothing?:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aw, I was going to post a half-naked, mangy, leathery, abused animal/creature/thing…

Doesn't stop me liking it, of course, the poor Woobie. I'm sure this Foundation is big enough for the two of us.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes it should Boa Noah. Thanks for the input, now edited for such a change.

EDIT: Also, I fixed the skull/face thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a bit unpolished, but promising. I'm a sucker for sad SCP's, and this one is certainly that. +1, with hope of further refinement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
poorly healed stabbing injuries

Shouldn't that be 'puncture wounds'?

apart from on the face which is bald.

That's unnecessary as you previously stated that it's face is a skull.

Overall it's okay, nothing great or special but it does have potential. The tone seems a little too casual for my taste but that's easily rectified.

item.title: It's Back!
item.content:encoded:
After making edits to the test log, and adding a brief history of recovery I've re-posted this SCP.

Note: As someone downvoted this last time as the claimed by posting a SCP about animal abuse meant I was for animal abuse in real life, I want you all to know that is not true. It's known as adding nightmare fuel. If a SCP scares you, you should upvote not downvote. We're a horror fiction site.
